pairs.question: What was the Seattle SuperSonics' record in the 2004–05 NBA season?
pairs.answer: The Seattle SuperSonics finished the 2004–05 season with a 52–30 record.

pairs.question: Which players from the 2004–05 SuperSonics team were selected to the 2005 NBA All-Star Game?
pairs.answer: Ray Allen and Rashard Lewis were both voted to play in the 2005 NBA All-Star Game.

pairs.question: How did the SuperSonics perform in the 2005 NBA playoffs?
pairs.answer: The SuperSonics defeated the Sacramento Kings in the first round (5 games) but lost in the second round to the San Antonio Spurs (6 games).

pairs.question: What major change occurred for the SuperSonics after the 2004–05 season?
pairs.answer: After the season, the SuperSonics relocated to Oklahoma City in 2008 and became the Oklahoma City Thunder.

pairs.question: Who was the head coach of the SuperSonics during the 2004–05 season, and where did he go afterward?
pairs.answer: Nate McMillan was the head coach during the 2004–05 season and left to become the head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers.

pairs.question: What new uniforms did the SuperSonics introduce for the 2004–05 season?
pairs.answer: The SuperSonics added new yellow alternate road uniforms that remained in use until 2008.

pairs.question: In the 2004–05 season, how many games did the SuperSonics win in their first 20 games?
pairs.answer: The SuperSonics won 17 of their first 20 games during the 2004–05 season.
